purpose: Track coding-model performance on bounded CodeLens RN refactor slices so future agents can choose the right worker model and improve prompts.

ratingScale.ten: Near-perfect worker result: exact scope discipline, explicit exceptions, complete verification, no reviewer interpretation needed.
ratingScale.strong: Scoped, compatible, verified, easy to review.
ratingScale.medium: Mostly correct but needs reviewer cleanup or leaves local naming/test gaps.
ratingScale.weak: Useful fragments, but missed important boundaries or required substantial correction.
ratingScale.dangerous: Touched forbidden areas, risked data loss, weakened tests, or made broad uncontrolled edits.

tagTaxonomy.projectTags: Specific to CodeLens RN or the ontology-profile refactor branch.
tagTaxonomy.stackTags: Transferable within a technical stack, such as TypeScript, React Native, Drizzle, SQLite, Vitest, Expo, Zustand.
tagTaxonomy.generalCodingTags: Transferable across stacks, including Python, backend services, CLI tools, frontend apps, and data pipelines.
tagTaxonomy.riskTags: Areas where the model should be avoided or needs stronger review.

generalCodingTagDefinitions.bounded-refactor: Can make small controlled changes without expanding scope.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.compatibility-boundary: Can preserve old/public/persistence API boundaries while improving internal code.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.terminology-rename: Can rename concepts consistently when given clear old/new vocabulary.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.interface-propagation: Can propagate type/interface changes through call sites.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.test-discipline: Runs relevant checks and uses failures constructively.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.scope-control: Avoids unrelated files and broad refactors.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.reporting: Produces a useful final report with what changed, what stayed legacy, and verification.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.persistence-risk: Needs extra caution around DB, migrations, import/export, or data-loss boundaries.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.alias-migration: Can add a preferred new field or API while preserving and documenting a legacy alias.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.regression-guarding: Can add targeted tests or source guards for known failure modes.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.source-guards: Can write source-level architecture guards where runtime tests would be too heavy.
generalCodingTagDefinitions.prompt-style-aware: Result includes enough prompt-shape metadata to compare fairly with other model runs.

evaluations.slice: Rename graph-owned ontology type naming from conceptType to typeNodeId while preserving LearningConcept.conceptType compatibility.
strengths: Stayed inside the graph boundary.
Preserved the domain compatibility boundary: LearningConcept.conceptType maps into GraphNode.typeNodeId.
Did not touch persistence, backup, codecs, or promotion.
Ran targeted tests and full suite.
Produced a small, reviewable diff.
projectSpecificStrengths: Understood the CodeLens ontology-profile compatibility boundary between LearningConcept and GraphNode.
Used the existing graph feature boundaries instead of starting a wider app rename.
Kept current coding-profile behavior unchanged.
generalizedCodingStrengths: Good at bounded terminology refactors.
Good at preserving an explicit compatibility boundary while renaming internals.
Good at small interface/prop updates when tests and allowed write scope are clear.
Reliable enough to run as a worker on another repo for narrow, well-tested feature-local cleanup.
generalizedStackStrengths: Handled TypeScript interface/property renames cleanly across feature code and tests.
Worked well with React/React Native prop and component naming updates.
Used Vitest feedback effectively for a small feature slice.
Good candidate for TypeScript frontend or React Native feature-local naming cleanup.
misses: Left helper name conceptTypeColor in graph internals.
Left warning/test wording saying unknown concept type.
reviewerFixes: Renamed conceptTypeColor to typeNodeColor.
Changed warning/test wording to unknown ontology type node.
verification: Full test suite: 315/315 passed across 49 files
recommendedUse: Compatibility-preserving type/interface renames with clear tests
avoidFor: Unbounded architecture planning until it proves itself on more slices
Data migration or backup/import logic without extra review
promptImprovements: Add explicit instruction to rename helper names, warning text, and test descriptions when the slice is about terminology.
Ask the worker to run rg for legacy terms inside the allowed write scope before final report.

promptImprovements: Keep using strict bounded ticket prompts for Kimi K2.6 implementation work.
When behavior is easy to unit test, require an actual behavior test in addition to source guards.

Record promptStyle and evaluationMeaning so future HR comparisons are fair.

generalizedLessons.lesson: For naming refactors, require a final rg scan for the old terminology inside the allowed write scope.
For persistence or backup work, require tests against the real runtime boundary shape, not only codec/domain-shaped objects.
Any import/restore task must validate and decode all incoming data before clearing current data.
A legacy name can remain only when it crosses a real compatibility, persistence, or public API boundary. Local internals should move to the new vocabulary.

Record project-specific strengths separately from generalized stack strengths and generalized coding strengths so future model choice can transfer to other repositories without overfitting to one codebase.
Record promptStyle, taskType, promptStrictness, contextProvided, allowedWriteScopeStyle, evaluationMeaning, and promptAdaptationNotes so future model comparisons account for prompt shape.

For preferred-new-field plus legacy-alias changes, specify preferred-only, legacy-only, and both-present behavior. Require a direct behavior test when cheap; source guards are acceptable only when they match repo guard style.

promptStyleTaxonomy.strict bounded ticket: Exact context files, explicit write scope, compatibility boundaries, verification commands, rg/search requirements, and final report format.
promptStyleTaxonomy.test-only guard: Production code is out of scope; worker adds regression or architecture guards for a known failure mode.
promptStyleTaxonomy.architecture/doc guard: Documentation and source-level architecture guard hardening after implementation slices.
promptStyleTaxonomy.loose exploratory: Read-only or planning-oriented investigation with more autonomy; avoid for destructive or persistence work.

promptAdaptationRules: Record promptStyle, taskType, promptStrictness, contextProvided, allowedWriteScopeStyle, evaluationMeaning, and promptAdaptationNotes for new evaluations.
Use strict bounded tickets for implementation work by cheap/open-weight workers until a model proves broader autonomous planning.
For compatibility aliases, require preferred-only, legacy-only, and both-present behavior to be specified and tested or guarded.
For persistence-adjacent work, require raw boundary-shape tests and validate-before-wipe behavior, or keep the worker draft-only.
